name: RABBIT-CAGE-24
title: plugin-stale alert persists entire session after /reload-plugins: no within-session marker clear mechanism
description: When build.sh writes .rabbit-plugins-stale, sync-check.sh alerts on every Stop until the marker is cleared. The marker is only cleared by session-init.sh (session start, /clear, /compact, restart). There is no way to clear it when the user runs /reload-plugins within an active session — Claude Code exposes no hook event for /reload-plugins execution, and custom commands cannot shadow built-ins. Result: the user runs /reload-plugins (skills actually reloaded) but the alert keeps firing for the rest of the session. A mechanism to clear the marker within-session is needed.

history.note: Official Claude Code docs confirm: local .claude/skills/ files have live change detection — changes take effect within the current session without restarting. /reload-plugins is irrelevant for project-level skills. Once invoked, skill content is cached in the conversation; re-invoking the skill reads the fresh file. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/skills (Live change detection + Skill content lifecycle sections). The .rabbit-plugins-stale mechanism was solving a non-problem and prescribing the wrong command. Fix: replace with .rabbit-skills-updated self-clearing one-time notification naming updated skills.
